definition: argparse is Python's standard-library module for defining and parsing command-line interfaces: you declare the arguments a program accepts and it parses sys.argv, validates types, applies defaults, and auto-generates help.
layman: It turns the words you type after a script's name into clean, validated Python variables — and gives you a --help screen for free.
content:
## argparse

Instead of poking at `sys.argv` by hand, you declare what your program accepts and let argparse parse, validate and document it.

```python
import argparse

parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Generate embeddings for a folder")
parser.add_argument("path", help="folder of documents")          # positional (required)
parser.add_argument("--model", default="text-embedding-3-small")  # optional with default
parser.add_argument("--batch-size", type=int, default=64)          # typed
parser.add_argument("--dry-run", action="store_true")             # boolean flag

args = parser.parse_args()
print(args.path, args.model, args.batch_size, args.dry_run)
```

### Key building blocks

| Feature | What it does |
|---------|--------------|
| positional arg | required, order-based (`path`) |
| `--option` | optional flag, accessed as `args.option` |
| `type=int` | converts and validates the value |
| `default=` | value used when the flag is omitted |
| `choices=[...]` | restricts to allowed values |
| `action="store_true"` | boolean on/off flag |
| `required=True` | makes an option mandatory |
| subparsers | nested subcommands (`tool ingest`, `tool eval`) |

`parse_args()` returns a `Namespace`; dashes in `--batch-size` become `args.batch_size`. Invalid input prints a usage message and exits with a non-zero code.

### Why this matters for Gen AI

Most Gen AI workflows ship as CLIs: ingest documents, run evaluations, batch-generate completions. argparse lets you expose model name, temperature, input/output paths and flags like `--dry-run` consistently, with built-in `--help` and validation.
keyPoints: Declare arguments with add_argument; positional args are required, --options are optional.
Use type= to convert/validate, default= for fallbacks, choices= to restrict values.
action='store_true' creates boolean flags; --batch-size becomes args.batch_size.
parse_args() returns a Namespace and auto-generates --help.
Subparsers add nested subcommands for multi-command tools.
